container_info.description:
**Test Responsive Web Application**

This project aims to develop a modern, responsive web application featuring a Login page, Home page, and an admin section. The application will provide a seamless user experience across devices, ensuring accessibility and usability for both end-users and administrators. The admin section will enable management of users and content, while the login functionality will ensure secure access control. The project will follow best practices in web development, focusing on scalability, maintainability, and security.
container_info.reasoning: The project describes a modern responsive web application (SPA-like) with Login, Home and admin sections and a frontend container named WebFrontend. The container includes nodejs, npm, yarn, typescript and create-react-app as preinstalled tooling, which strongly indicates a JavaScript SPA workflow. React (via create-react-app) is a common, lightweight choice for responsive frontends and matches the available tools in the image.
requirements: npm or yarn (package manager) -- npm and yarn available; use one
create-react-app or Vite CLI (project scaffolding) -- create-react-app present; Vite optional
typescript (optional) if using TSX; typescript is preinstalled
minimal package.json with react, react-dom and react-scripts (or Vite dev server) dependencies
headless dev server: react-scripts start (or vite) to run in dev mode
build tool: react-scripts build (or vite build) for production artifacts
lightweight test runner: jest (preinstalled) with a simple smoke test
basic lint/format: eslint and prettier (preinstalled) optional for dev quality
environment variables: NODE_ENV and PORT configured for headless operation
no external databases required for frontend-only operation (use localStorage or mocked API)
pm2 or nodemon optional for process management in container (pm2 preinstalled)
container_info.dockerfile_summary: OS: Ubuntu 24.04 (Debian family), Package Manager: apt-get, Sudo: Present (NOPASSWD), Preinstalled: git, curl, wget, python3, python3-pip, nodejs, npm, build-essential, postgresql, mysql-server, mongodb-org, redis-server, dotnet-sdk-8.0, uvicorn, celery, redis, requests, beautifulsoup4, sphinx, mkdocs, pylint, flask, awscli, boto3, yarn, typescript, @vue/cli, @angular/cli, create-react-app, express-generator, nodemon, pm2, eslint, prettier, webpack, jest

steps.name: Environment: persist NODE env and validate node/npm
steps.description: Create an idempotent /etc/profile.d/node_env.sh to persist NODE_ENV (default if unset), PORT and HOST for headless operation and add precomputed npm global bin to PATH (computed at install time). Validate node and npm presence and minimum versions. Create workspace and .setup_logs and record node/npm versions. Export env locally in-script so subsequent commands during the run observe them.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/test-responsive-web-application-43742-43771/WebFrontend"
PROFILE=/etc/profile.d/node_env.sh
LOGDIR="$WORKSPACE/.setup_logs"
MIN_NODE_MAJOR=16
MIN_NPM_MAJOR=8
mkdir -p "$WORKSPACE" "$LOGDIR"
command -v node >/dev/null || { echo "node not found" >&2; exit 2; }
command -v npm >/dev/null || { echo "npm not found" >&2; exit 2; }
NODE_VER=$(node -v | sed 's/^v//')
NPM_VER=$(npm -v)
NODE_MAJOR=${NODE_VER%%.*}
NPM_MAJOR=${NPM_VER%%.*}
if [ "${NODE_MAJOR:-0}" -lt "$MIN_NODE_MAJOR" ]; then echo "node version $NODE_VER < required $MIN_NODE_MAJOR" >&2; exit 3; fi
if [ "${NPM_MAJOR:-0}" -lt "$MIN_NPM_MAJOR" ]; then echo "npm version $NPM_VER < required $MIN_NPM_MAJOR" >&2; exit 4; fi
# Compute npm prefix once (avoid invoking npm at shell startup)
NPM_PREFIX=""
if command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then NPM_PREFIX=$(npm config get prefix 2>/dev/null || true); fi
TMP=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$TMP"' EXIT
cat > "$TMP" <<'EOF'
# Auto-generated by WebFrontend environment setup
export NODE_ENV="${NODE_ENV:-development}"
export PORT="3000"
export HOST="0.0.0.0"
# Precomputed npm global bin (populated at install time)
_PRECOMP_PREFIX="_NPM_PREFIX_PLACEHOLDER_"
if [ -n "${_PRECOMP_PREFIX}" ] && [ -d "${_PRECOMP_PREFIX}/bin" ]; then
  case ":$PATH:" in
    *":${_PRECOMP_PREFIX}/bin:"*) :;;
    *) export PATH="${_PRECOMP_PREFIX}/bin:$PATH" ;;
  esac
fi
unset _PRECOMP_PREFIX
EOF
ESC_PREFIX=$(printf '%s' "$NPM_PREFIX" | sed "s:':\\':g")
if [ -z "$ESC_PREFIX" ]; then
  sed -i "s/_NPM_PREFIX_PLACEHOLDER_//g" "$TMP"
else
  sed -i "s/_NPM_PREFIX_PLACEHOLDER_/$ESC_PREFIX/g" "$TMP"
fi
if ! sudo test -f "$PROFILE" || ! sudo cmp -s "$TMP" "$PROFILE"; then
  sudo tee "$PROFILE" >/dev/null < "$TMP" && sudo chmod 0644 "$PROFILE"
fi
# record versions
echo "node=$NODE_VER" > "$LOGDIR/versions.env"
echo "npm=$NPM_VER" >> "$LOGDIR/versions.env"
# export locally for this run
export NODE_ENV=${NODE_ENV:-development}
export PORT=3000
export HOST=0.0.0.0
exit 0

steps.name: Scaffolding: create-react-app scaffold if project missing
steps.description: If package.json is missing in the workspace, non-interactively scaffold a Create React App project using the installed create-react-app binary and --use-npm. Verify installed create-react-app version is compatible (best-effort) and refuse to run in non-empty workspaces except for a safe whitelist (.setup_logs, README, .gitignore, .git). Log output to .setup_logs/create_react_app.log and validate that package.json contains start, build and test scripts.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/test-responsive-web-application-43742-43771/WebFrontend"
LOGDIR="$WORKSPACE/.setup_logs"
LOGFILE="$LOGDIR/create_react_app.log"
mkdir -p "$WORKSPACE" "$LOGDIR"
cd "$WORKSPACE"
# If package.json exists, skip
if [ -f package.json ]; then echo "scaffold: package.json exists, skipping" >"$LOGFILE"; exit 0; fi
# Safe whitelist
SAFE_LIST=(".git" "README" "README.md" ".gitignore" ".setup_logs")
for f in $(ls -A); do
  ok=0
  for s in "${SAFE_LIST[@]}"; do [ "$f" = "$s" ] && ok=1 || true; done
  if [ $ok -eq 0 ]; then echo "scaffold: workspace contains unexpected file '$f', aborting" >"$LOGFILE"; exit 5; fi
done
if ! command -v create-react-app >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "scaffold: create-react-app not found" >"$LOGFILE"; exit 6; fi
CRAVERSION=$(create-react-app --version 2>/dev/null || true)
CRA_MAJOR=$(echo "$CRAVERSION" | awk -F. '{print $1}' 2>/dev/null || true)
if [ -z "$CRAVERSION" ] || [ -z "$CRA_MAJOR" ] || ! echo "$CRA_MAJOR" | grep -E '^[0-9]+$' >/dev/null 2>&1 || [ "$CRA_MAJOR" -lt 4 ]; then
  echo "scaffold: create-react-app version '$CRAVERSION' may be incompatible; aborting" >"$LOGFILE"; exit 7
fi
# scaffold
set +e
create-react-app . --use-npm >"$LOGFILE" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e
if [ $RC -ne 0 ]; then echo "scaffold: create-react-app failed (rc=$RC). See $LOGFILE" >&2; tail -n 200 "$LOGFILE" >&2 || true; exit 8; fi
# validate package.json scripts
if ! node -e "const fs=require('fs');const p=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('package.json'));const s=p.scripts||{}; if(!s.start||!s.build||!s.test){console.error('missing default scripts');process.exit(2);}" 2>>"$LOGFILE"; then
  echo 'scaffold: validation failed - missing start/build/test scripts' >>"$LOGFILE"
  exit 9
fi
echo "scaffold: create-react-app completed (version: $CRAVERSION)" >>"$LOGFILE"
exit 0

steps.name: Dependencies: install npm packages (npm ci preferred)
steps.description: Install project dependencies in workspace. Use npm ci when package-lock.json exists for deterministic installs; otherwise use npm i. Export NODE_ENV and PORT for the run. Record installed versions for react/react-dom/react-scripts and write logs to .setup_logs/npm_install.log. Create minimal .eslintrc.json, .prettierrc.json and .gitignore if missing and add lint/format npm scripts if absent.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/test-responsive-web-application-43742-43771/WebFrontend"
LOGDIR="$WORKSPACE/.setup_logs"
LOGFILE="$LOGDIR/npm_install.log"
mkdir -p "$WORKSPACE" "$LOGDIR"
cd "$WORKSPACE"
[ -f package.json ] || { echo "install: package.json missing in workspace ($WORKSPACE)" >&2; exit 3; }
export NODE_ENV=${NODE_ENV:-development}
export PORT=${PORT:-3000}
# Record node/npm
node -e "const fs=require('fs');fs.writeFileSync(process.env.PWD+'/.setup_logs/node_npm_versions.json',JSON.stringify({node:process.version, npm:require('child_process').execSync('npm --version').toString().trim()},null,2))" || true
if ! command -v npm >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "npm not found" >&2; exit 4; fi
if [ -f package-lock.json ]; then
  npm ci --no-audit --no-fund >"$LOGFILE" 2>&1 || { echo "npm ci failed, see $LOGFILE" >&2; tail -n 200 "$LOGFILE" >&2 || true; exit 5; }
else
  npm i --no-audit --no-fund >"$LOGFILE" 2>&1 || { echo "npm install failed, see $LOGFILE" >&2; tail -n 200 "$LOGFILE" >&2 || true; exit 6; }
fi
# record installed package versions
node -e "const fs=require('fs');const out={};['react','react-dom','react-scripts'].forEach(n=>{try{out[n]=require('./node_modules/'+n+'/package.json').version}catch(e){out[n]=null}});fs.writeFileSync(process.env.PWD+'/.setup_logs/installed_versions.json',JSON.stringify(out,null,2));" || true
# lint/format configs
[ -f .eslintrc.json ] || cat > .eslintrc.json <<'EOF'
{ "extends": ["react-app", "eslint:recommended"], "rules": {} }
EOF
[ -f .prettierrc.json ] || cat > .prettierrc.json <<'EOF'
{ "singleQuote": true, "trailingComma": "es5" }
EOF
[ -f .gitignore ] || cat > .gitignore <<'EOF'
node_modules
build
.env
EOF
# add npm scripts if missing
node -e "const fs=require('fs');const p=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('package.json'));p.scripts=p.scripts||{};let changed=false;if(!p.scripts.lint){p.scripts.lint='eslint src --ext .js,.jsx';changed=true;}if(!p.scripts.format){p.scripts.format='prettier --write \"src/**/*.{js,jsx,json,css}\"';changed=true;}if(changed)fs.writeFileSync('package.json',JSON.stringify(p,null,2));" || true
echo '{"status":"ok","installed_on":"'"$(date --iso-8601=seconds)"'"}' > "$LOGDIR/install_status.json"
exit 0

steps.name: Testing: add Jest smoke test and run tests non-interactively
steps.description: Add a minimal Jest smoke test under src/__tests__/smoke.test.js if missing. Preserve existing test script in package.json; otherwise set a non-interactive react-scripts test. Run tests with CI=1 and capture logs to .setup_logs/jest.log. Detect jest version via command or node_modules inspection (no npx network fetch).
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/test-responsive-web-application-43742-43771/WebFrontend"
LOGDIR="$WORKSPACE/.setup_logs"
LOGFILE="$LOGDIR/jest.log"
mkdir -p "$WORKSPACE" "$LOGDIR"
cd "$WORKSPACE"
[ -f package.json ] || { echo "testing: package.json missing" >&2; exit 3; }
mkdir -p src/__tests__
if [ ! -f src/__tests__/smoke.test.js ]; then
  cat > src/__tests__/smoke.test.js <<'EOF'
test('smoke: true is true', () => { expect(true).toBe(true); });
EOF
fi
# preserve existing test script; if missing add non-interactive react-scripts test
node -e "const fs=require('fs');const p=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('package.json'));p.scripts=p.scripts||{}; if(!p.scripts.test) p.scripts.test='react-scripts test --watchAll=false --silent'; fs.writeFileSync('package.json',JSON.stringify(p,null,2));"
export CI=1
{
  echo "node=$(node -v)"
  if command -v jest >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "jest=$(jest --version 2>/dev/null || echo unknown)"
  elif [ -f node_modules/jest/package.json ]; then
    node -e "console.log('jest='+require('./node_modules/jest/package.json').version)"
  else
    echo "jest=not-found"
  fi
} >> "$LOGFILE" 2>&1 || true
npm test >"$LOGFILE" 2>&1 || { echo "testing: tests failed, see $LOGFILE" >&2; tail -n 200 "$LOGFILE" >&2 || true; exit 4; }
exit 0

steps.name: Build: produce production artifacts with react-scripts build
steps.description: Run 'npm run build' to produce production artifacts. This step depends on scaffolding and dependencies; if package.json is missing it will fail fast and report the missing artifact. Set CI=1, capture build logs to .setup_logs/build.log and verify build/index.html exists and contains a root div.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/test-responsive-web-application-43742-43771/WebFrontend"
LOGDIR="$WORKSPACE/.setup_logs"
LOGFILE="$LOGDIR/build.log"
mkdir -p "$WORKSPACE" "$LOGDIR"
cd "$WORKSPACE"
[ -f package.json ] || { echo "build: package.json missing in workspace ($WORKSPACE)" >&2; exit 3; }
export CI=1
# ensure build script exists
node -e "const fs=require('fs');const p=JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('package.json'));p.scripts=p.scripts||{}; if(!p.scripts.build) p.scripts.build='react-scripts build'; fs.writeFileSync('package.json',JSON.stringify(p,null,2));"
npm run build >"$LOGFILE" 2>&1 || { echo "build: build failed, see $LOGFILE" >&2; tail -n 200 "$LOGFILE" >&2 || true; exit 4; }
if [ ! -f build/index.html ]; then echo "build: missing build/index.html" >&2; tail -n 200 "$LOGFILE" >&2 || true; exit 5; fi
if ! grep -q "<div id=\"root\"\|<div id='root'" build/index.html >/dev/null 2>&1; then echo "build: index.html missing root div" >&2; exit 6; fi
echo "build: artifact produced" >> "$LOGFILE"
exit 0

echo "build: artifact produced" >> "$LOGFILE"
exit 0

steps.summary: Executed the build step script for producing production artifacts with react-scripts. Created a concise, idempotent .init/build.sh that implements the provided script exactly, with strict error handling, CI=1 export, logging to .setup_logs/build.log, checks for package.json, ensures a build script exists in package.json, runs npm run build redirecting stdout/stderr to the log file, and validates that build/index.html exists and contains a root div. The script exits with clear, non-zero exit codes on missing package.json (3), build failure (4), missing build/index.html (5), and missing root div (6). The workspace path is the container-provided workspace and all file operations use absolute paths derived from it. No architect escalation required.
steps.qa_summary: The engineer reports a successful, idempotent build step implementation: .init/build.sh enforces set -euo pipefail, exports CI=1, checks for package.json and a build script, runs npm run build logging to .setup_logs/build.log, and validates build/index.html contains a root div. Clear non-zero exit codes for failure modes were defined and no critical errors or questions were left. Core objective (produce production artifacts and validate index.html) is met according to the provided summary.

steps.name: Validation: build -> start deterministic static server -> probe -> stop
steps.description: Validate the full environment: ensure build exists (will call build if missing), start a deterministic static server (prefer node_modules/.bin/serve; otherwise create an embedded static-server.js), probe HTTP endpoint until responsive, capture logs under .setup_logs/react_start.log, then stop the server cleanly. Use pm2 with a unique name if available, otherwise PID-based background process. This step depends on environment, scaffolding, dependencies, testing, and build.
steps.script_code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
WORKSPACE="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/test-responsive-web-application-43742-43771/WebFrontend"
LOGDIR="$WORKSPACE/.setup_logs"
LOGFILE="$LOGDIR/react_start.log"
PIDFILE="$LOGDIR/devserver.pid"
PM2_NAME="webfrontend-serve"
mkdir -p "$WORKSPACE" "$LOGDIR"
cd "$WORKSPACE"
export PORT=${PORT:-3000}
export HOST=${HOST:-0.0.0.0}
export NODE_ENV=production
export BROWSER=none
export CI=1
# ensure build exists, attempt to build if missing
if [ ! -f build/index.html ]; then
  echo "validation: build missing, running build" >"$LOGFILE"
  npm run build >>"$LOGFILE" 2>&1 || { echo "validation: build failed, see $LOGFILE" >&2; tail -n 200 "$LOGFILE" >&2 || true; exit 6; }
fi
# determine start command
START_CMD=""
if [ -x "node_modules/.bin/serve" ]; then
  START_CMD="node_modules/.bin/serve -s build -l ${PORT}"
else
  cat > "$WORKSPACE/static-server.js" <<'EOF'
const http=require('http'),fs=require('fs'),path=require('path');const port=process.env.PORT||3000;const root=path.join(__dirname,'build');const mime={'html':'text/html','js':'application/javascript','css':'text/css','json':'application/json','png':'image/png','jpg':'image/jpeg'};http.createServer((req,res)=>{let p=path.join(root,req.url.split('?')[0]); if(p.endsWith('/')) p=path.join(p,'index.html'); fs.stat(p,(e,s)=>{ if(e){ res.statusCode=404; res.end('not found'); return;} const ext=path.extname(p).slice(1); res.setHeader('Content-Type',mime[ext]||'application/octet-stream'); fs.createReadStream(p).pipe(res); });}).listen(port,()=>console.log('static server listening on '+port));
EOF
  START_CMD="node $WORKSPACE/static-server.js"
fi
set +e
if command -v pm2 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  pm2 delete "$PM2_NAME" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  pm2 start --name "$PM2_NAME" --no-autorestart -- $START_CMD >"$LOGFILE" 2>&1 || true
  sleep 1
  PID=$(pm2 pid "$PM2_NAME" 2>/dev/null || true)
  if [ -n "$PID" ] && [ "$PID" != "-" ]; then echo "$PID" > "$PIDFILE"; fi
else
  nohup setsid sh -c "$START_CMD" >"$LOGFILE" 2>&1 & echo $! > "$PIDFILE"
fi
set -e
TRIES=0
MAX=60
until curl -sSf "http://localhost:${PORT}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || [ $TRIES -ge $MAX ]; do sleep 1; TRIES=$((TRIES+1)); done
if curl -sSf "http://localhost:${PORT}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "validation: server responded on port ${PORT}" >> "$LOGFILE"
  tail -n 100 "$LOGFILE" || true
  # cleanup
  if command -v pm2 >/dev/null 2>&1; then pm2 delete "$PM2_NAME" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; fi
  if [ -f "$PIDFILE" ]; then
    PID=$(cat "$PIDFILE" || true)
    if [ -n "$PID" ] && kill -0 "$PID" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      kill -TERM "$PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
      sleep 1
      kill -KILL "$PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
    rm -f "$PIDFILE" || true
  fi
  exit 0
else
  echo "validation: server did not respond within ${MAX}s" >&2
  tail -n 200 "$LOGFILE" >&2 || true
  if command -v pm2 >/dev/null 2>&1; then pm2 delete "$PM2_NAME" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; fi
  if [ -f "$PIDFILE" ]; then PID=$(cat "$PIDFILE" || true); [ -n "$PID" ] && kill -TERM "$PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; fi
  exit 7
fi

output_scripts.name: validation
output_scripts.code:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
# Validation: build -> start deterministic static server -> probe -> stop
WORKSPACE="/home/kavia/workspace/code-generation/test-responsive-web-application-43742-43771/WebFrontend"
LOGDIR="$WORKSPACE/.setup_logs"
LOGFILE="$LOGDIR/react_start.log"
PIDFILE="$LOGDIR/devserver.pid"
PM2_NAME="webfrontend-serve"
mkdir -p "$WORKSPACE" "$LOGDIR"
cd "$WORKSPACE"
export PORT=${PORT:-3000}
export HOST=${HOST:-0.0.0.0}
export NODE_ENV=${NODE_ENV:-production}
export BROWSER=none
export CI=1
# Ensure build exists; run build if missing
if [ ! -f "$WORKSPACE/build/index.html" ]; then
  echo "validation: build missing, running build" >"$LOGFILE"
  npm run build >>"$LOGFILE" 2>&1 || { echo "validation: build failed, see $LOGFILE" >&2; tail -n 200 "$LOGFILE" >&2 || true; exit 6; }
fi
# Determine start command: prefer local serve if executable, else create embedded server
START_CMD=""
if [ -x "$WORKSPACE/node_modules/.bin/serve" ]; then
  START_CMD="$WORKSPACE/node_modules/.bin/serve -s $WORKSPACE/build -l ${PORT}"
else
  cat > "$WORKSPACE/static-server.js" <<'EOF'
const http=require('http'),fs=require('fs'),path=require('path');const port=process.env.PORT||3000;const root=path.join(__dirname,'build');const mime={'html':'text/html','js':'application/javascript','css':'text/css','json':'application/json','png':'image/png','jpg':'image/jpeg','svg':'image/svg+xml'};http.createServer((req,res)=>{let url=req.url.split('?')[0]; if(url.includes('..')){res.statusCode=400;res.end('bad request');return;} let p=path.join(root, url); if(req.url.endsWith('/')) p=path.join(p,'index.html'); fs.stat(p,(e,s)=>{ if(e){ res.statusCode=404; res.end('not found'); return;} const ext=path.extname(p).slice(1); res.setHeader('Content-Type',mime[ext]||'application/octet-stream'); fs.createReadStream(p).pipe(res); });}).listen(port,()=>console.log('static server listening on '+port));
EOF
  START_CMD="node $WORKSPACE/static-server.js"
fi
# Start server under pm2 if available, else background process with PID file
set +e
if command -v pm2 >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  pm2 delete "$PM2_NAME" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
  # Use sh -c so START_CMD is interpreted correctly
  pm2 start --name "$PM2_NAME" --no-autorestart -- sh -c "$START_CMD" >"$LOGFILE" 2>&1 || true
  sleep 1
  PID=$(pm2 pid "$PM2_NAME" 2>/dev/null || true)
  if [ -n "$PID" ] && [ "$PID" != "-" ]; then echo "$PID" > "$PIDFILE"; fi
else
  nohup setsid sh -c "$START_CMD" >"$LOGFILE" 2>&1 & echo $! > "$PIDFILE"
fi
set -e
# Probe until responsive
TRIES=0
MAX=60
until curl -sSf "http://localhost:${PORT}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || [ $TRIES -ge $MAX ]; do sleep 1; TRIES=$((TRIES+1)); done
if curl -sSf "http://localhost:${PORT}" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "validation: server responded on port ${PORT}" >> "$LOGFILE"
  tail -n 100 "$LOGFILE" || true
  # Cleanup
  if command -v pm2 >/dev/null 2>&1; then pm2 delete "$PM2_NAME" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; fi
  if [ -f "$PIDFILE" ]; then
    PID=$(cat "$PIDFILE" || true)
    if [ -n "$PID" ] && kill -0 "$PID" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
      kill -TERM "$PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
      sleep 1
      kill -KILL "$PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true
    fi
    rm -f "$PIDFILE" || true
  fi
  exit 0
else
  echo "validation: server did not respond within ${MAX}s" >&2
  tail -n 200 "$LOGFILE" >&2 || true
  if command -v pm2 >/dev/null 2>&1; then pm2 delete "$PM2_NAME" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; fi
  if [ -f "$PIDFILE" ]; then PID=$(cat "$PIDFILE" || true); [ -n "$PID" ] && kill -TERM "$PID" >/dev/null 2>&1 || true; fi
  exit 7
fi

steps.summary: Executed the validation step: created an idempotent validation script that ensures a production build exists (runs npm run build if missing), starts a deterministic static server (prefers node_modules/.bin/serve; otherwise writes an embedded static-server.js), probes the HTTP endpoint until responsive (with a 60s timeout), captures logs to .setup_logs/react_start.log, and stops the server cleanly. The script uses pm2 with a unique name when available, otherwise falls back to a PID-based background process. It writes a PID file (.setup_logs/devserver.pid), handles cleanup of pm2 process or PID process, and returns exit code 0 on success or non-zero with tail of logs on failure. The script follows required structure (#!/usr/bin/env bash, set -euo pipefail), uses the provided workspace path, exports NODE_ENV/PORT/HOST/BROWSER/CI for headless operation, and is non-interactive and idempotent. I did not require architect help.
steps.qa_summary: The engineer reported a successful, idempotent validation script that ensures a production build (runs npm run build if missing), starts a deterministic static server (prefers node_modules/.bin/serve, falls back to embedded static-server.js), probes the HTTP endpoint with a 60s timeout, logs to .setup_logs/react_start.log, writes a PID file, and stops the server cleanly using pm2 when available or a PID-based background process. The script follows required structure and environment variable exports, is non-interactive, and returns appropriate exit codes. No critical errors or security issues were reported.

reasoning: Goal: produce a minimal, idempotent, non-interactive setup plan that prepares a headless React development workspace inside the existing running container. Architectural decisions: (1) Use preinstalled tools (node, npm, create-react-app, jest, eslint, prettier, pm2) — do not reinstall; (2) Follow the required workspace path and create/check package.json before build; (3) Keep steps minimal and one-per-category: environment, scaffolding, dependencies, testing, build, validation; (4) Make scaffolding explicit and permissive: if package.json absent, scaffold a CRA app non-interactively using the installed create-react-app; (5) Dependencies step uses npm ci when package-lock.json exists, otherwise npm i; (6) Testing step adds a Jest smoke test and runs tests non-interactively; (7) Build step now explicitly depends on scaffolding+dependencies and will fail fast with clear error if package.json missing; (8) Validation step builds if needed, starts a deterministic static server (prefer node_modules/.bin/serve, otherwise embedded static-server.js), probes the server, and stops it cleanly (pm2 if available, otherwise PID-based). All scripts follow the required shebang, strict error handling, non-interactive behavior, use the authoritative workspace path, write logs under .setup_logs, and persist minimal environment via /etc/profile.d/node_env.sh in the environment step. This plan directly addresses the engineer feedback (build blocked due to missing package.json) by ensuring scaffolding and dependency installation run before build and by making the build step dependent on those steps. Steps are idempotent and sized to be executed by an autonomous engineer agent.

change_logs.message: Refined initial plan to address analyst feedback: modified step-environment-001 to safely write /etc/profile.d node env (use sudo tee, idempotent, add HOST, avoid destructive PATH changes) and added Node/npm version validation; updated step-scaffold-002 to avoid in-place CRA on non-empty dirs, log scaffold output to workspace .setup_logs, and validate generated package.json; corrected step-dependencies-003 script_name from 'build' to 'install', made npm installs idempotent and logged, and made eslint/prettier writes conditional; updated step-testing-004 to preserve CRA's test script (only set if missing), add smoke test, log jest output and run react-scripts test wrapper non-interactively; improved step-validation-005 to set HOST=0.0.0.0, choose runtime start verification (start dev server only), increase timeout, probe via curl, capture logs into .setup_logs, and reliably terminate process group. These changes address critical, high and medium feedback items for safety, idempotency, and observability.

Refined plan to address analyst feedback: (1) Modified step-environment-001 to use sudo tee with deferred npm prefix expansion, set file mode to 0644, add trap cleanup, and log versions; (2) Improved step-scaffold-002 workspace-empty detection, use the global create-react-app binary explicitly and log its version; (3) Updated step-dependencies-003 to prefer 'npm ci' when package-lock.json exists, validate installed package versions, and log them; (4) Updated step-testing-004 to run tests with CI=1 to avoid interactive watch mode and log node/jest versions; (5) Added step-build-006 (new) to run 'npm run build' and validate build artifacts; (6) Revised step-validation-005 to perform build->start->probe->stop, use nohup/setsid and pidfile for robust process management, and use pm2 as fallback to ensure reliable termination. These changes resolve critical issues (missing build step), HIGH items (profile write safety, workspace checks, non-interactive tests), and MEDIUM items (lockfile handling, robust start/stop).

Refined plan to address analyst feedback: (1) Modified step-environment-001 to avoid running 'npm' at shell startup, set PATH additions by persisting a computed prefix at install time (not at every shell login), set NODE_ENV only if unset, and export env locally within scripts for immediate effect; (2) Updated step-scaffold-002 to validate create-react-app version compatibility with Node/NPM and to allow .setup_logs and a documented safe-file list when deciding whether to scaffold; (3) Kept step-dependencies-003 largely unchanged but ensured local environment variables are exported so npm commands run with expected env; (4) Modified step-testing-004 to avoid npx usage for jest version checks and to use command -v jest or node_modules inspection; (5) Changed step-build-006 category from 'custom' to 'build' to align metadata; (6) Rewrote step-validation-005 to avoid npx network fetch: prefer node_modules/.bin/serve if present, otherwise write a tiny static-server.js into the workspace and start it via pm2 with a unique name (webfrontend-serve) or via nohup if pm2 is not desired; ensure pm2 start/stop uses the named process only and do not run 'pm2 delete all'; added robust PID/group cleanup and traps. These changes address the HIGH and MEDIUM recommendations about npx brittleness, profile write issues, create-react-app compatibility, pm2 safety, and idempotency.

Refined plan to resolve the blocking build issue: (1) Ensured scaffolding (step-scaffold-002) is run prior to build and made explicit that scaffolding will create a CRA project if package.json is missing; (2) Added/clarified dependencies step (step-dependencies-003) to install packages (npm ci or npm i) before build; (3) Updated build step (step-build-006) to depend on scaffolding and dependencies and to fail fast with clear errors if package.json is still missing; (4) Preserved and kept environment, testing, and validation steps but updated validation to call build if missing and to start/stop the server deterministically. These changes address the DevOps Engineer's blocking feedback by guaranteeing the workspace is initialized and dependencies installed before attempting a build.
